headline: Operation Endgame gutted the SocGholish fake-update network while ransomware crews sharpened their own endpoint-killing tools and a USB-borne crypto-clipper spread across Windows.

emerging_trends.text: Ransomware-as-a-service gangs now build and hand affiliates dedicated tooling like GentleKiller that disables hundreds of endpoint defenses before encryption ever starts.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Gentlemen RaaS Arms Affiliates and Halts Mackay Sugar

emerging_trends.text: Malware authors weaponize AI from both ends, planting fake nuclear-weapon text to derail automated code analysis even as crypto-clipper crews enlist AI narrators to hawk their wares.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Malware Embeds Forbidden Text to Block AI Analysis

emerging_trends.text: International takedowns keep hammering the infrastructure behind fake-update lures, with Operation Endgame felling 106 SocGholish servers and scrubbing nearly 15,000 hijacked WordPress sites.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Operation Endgame Dismantles SocGholish and Evil Corp Infrastructure

topics.title: Windows Crypto-Clipper Spreads Over USB With Tor C2
topics.latest_developments: Microsoft's Defender team detailed a Windows cryptocurrency clipper running since February 2026 that propagates through USB LNK worms and polls a Tor hidden-service for commands, while Check Point traced a parallel clipper promoted through paid posts on legitimate news sites, fake VirusTotal comments, and AI-generated narrators.
topics.summary: Clippers silently swap a victim's copied wallet address for the attacker's, rerouting cryptocurrency payments, and this strain adds worm-like spread and a lightweight backdoor for persistent access. Users should verify pasted wallet addresses and block unknown USB devices.

sources.title: Microsoft Details Windows Clipper Malware Campaign Using USB LNK Worm and Tor-Based C2
Crypto Clipper uses Tor and worm-like propagation for persistence and control
Crypto Clipper Campaign Abuses Fake Reviews, AI Narrators, and VirusTotal Comments

topics.title: Operation Endgame Dismantles SocGholish and Evil Corp Infrastructure
topics.latest_developments: The Operation Endgame coalition, led by the Dutch National Police, seized 106 servers and domains and cleaned nearly 15,000 compromised WordPress sites that the Evil Corp-linked SocGholish botnet had turned into fake-software-update lures.
topics.summary: SocGholish delivers malware by pushing bogus browser and software update prompts on hacked websites, feeding follow-on access to Russia's Evil Corp cybercrime group. Site owners should confirm their WordPress installs are clean and patched.

sources.title: Law enforcement hits SocGholish: 106 servers down, 15,000 sites cleaned
Police cleans nearly 15,000 SocGholish-infected sites tied to Evil Corp

topics.title: Gentlemen RaaS Arms Affiliates and Halts Mackay Sugar
topics.latest_developments: ESET exposed how the Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service gang develops GentleKiller, an EDR-disabling toolkit that targets more than 400 security processes across 48 products and ships directly to affiliates, as Australian producer Mackay Sugar worked urgently to verify the gang's claim that it shut the company's harvesting and milling operations.
topics.summary: Gentlemen breaks from the usual model by building and maintaining endpoint-killing tools in-house rather than leaving that to affiliates, raising the success rate of its encryptions. A May 2026 internal leak confirmed the arrangement and named the gang's leader.

sources.title: GentleKiller targets more than 400 security processes across 48 products
Australian sugar producer works to restore operations as ransomware group claims attack

topics.title: F5 Ships Out-of-Band Patches for Critical NGINX Flaws
topics.latest_developments: F5 released out-of-band updates for multiple NGINX web server vulnerabilities, including two critical bugs that let remote, unauthenticated attackers force a restart and potentially execute arbitrary code.
topics.summary: NGINX runs a large share of the world's web servers, so the flaws expose internet-facing infrastructure to remote takeover. Administrators should apply the emergency fixes immediately.

sources.title: F5 issues out-of-band patches for critical NGINX vulnerabilities
F5 Patches Critical, High-Severity NGINX Vulnerabilities

topics.title: Klue OAuth Breach Feeds Icarus Salesforce Theft
topics.latest_developments: Market intelligence platform Klue suffered an OAuth breach that let the Icarus threat actors steal Salesforce CRM data from multiple organizations in an ongoing extortion campaign.
topics.summary: Icarus abuses connected third-party OAuth integrations to reach customers' Salesforce instances and exfiltrate sales and customer records for extortion. Affected firms should review and revoke Klue's Salesforce OAuth tokens and audit connected-app access.

sources.title: Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks

topics.title: Malware Embeds Forbidden Text to Block AI Analysis
topics.latest_developments: A malware developer began padding spyware with text about nuclear and biological weapons, hidden inside a large JavaScript block comment, to trip automated AI safety policies and stop AI tools from analyzing the code.
topics.summary: The fake policy-triggering content sits in a comment the runtime skips, with the real payload following in a try-eval wrapper, so the trick foils AI analysis without breaking execution. It signals attackers adapting to defenders' growing reliance on AI-driven reverse engineering.

sources.title: Embedding Forbidden Text in Spyware to Discourage AI Analysis

games.recap: Ryan O'Hearn knocks in career-high 6 runs as Pirates roll to 12-4 victory over Athletics

business_politics.title: U.S. and Iran Sign Deal, Funds and Ships Move
business_politics.latest_developments: Trump signed the memorandum of understanding with Iran at the G7 summit Thursday, pledged to release Tehran's frozen funds and ease banking and transport sanctions, and the U.S. Navy let more than a dozen ships pass to Iranian ports, lifting the blockade.
business_politics.summary: The accord halts the U.S.-Iran war and reopens the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for sanctions relief while leaving Iran's ballistic missiles and militias untouched; oil extended its losses as Hormuz traffic resumed, and U.S. pump prices slipped below $4 a gallon for the first time since March. Shipping executives warn the deal's language could let Tehran impose Hormuz transit fees after 60 days.

sources.title: US and Iran sign deal as Trump vows to release frozen funds and ease sanctions
Trump’s Deal Sidesteps Key Reasons He Went to War With Iran
Oil Extends Losses as Hormuz Traffic Increases

business.title: Kraft Heinz Reshapes Global Operations
business.latest_developments: Kraft Heinz, the Pittsburgh- and Chicago-headquartered food maker, shook up its global operating structure Thursday, the latest step in its continued corporate maneuvering.
business.summary: Kraft Heinz, the maker of Heinz ketchup and Kraft cheese and one of Pittsburgh's largest companies, reorganized how it runs its global business, a move watched closely by the region for its bearing on local jobs and headquarters operations.

sources.title: Kraft Heinz maneuvering continues with a shakeup to its global operating structure

business.title: Roaring Run Campers Sue Over Voided Memberships
business.latest_developments: Campers who bought lifetime memberships at the Roaring Run Resort in the Laurel Highlands filed a flurry of lawsuits after new owners voided the agreements and ordered them to vacate.
business.summary: The campers are suing both the previous and current owners of the Roaring Run Resort campground, seeking to be made whole after the lifetime memberships they had purchased went unhonored when the property changed hands.

sources.title: Pittsburgh-area campers head to court after lifetime memberships were voided by new owners

around_town.title: New Bus Service for the Waterfront
around_town.latest_developments: Pittsburgh Regional Transit will add new bus routes and stops serving the Waterfront shopping center in Homestead.
around_town.summary: The expanded service gives shoppers and workers new ways to reach the sprawling Waterfront retail complex along the Monongahela River, which long lacked direct transit access.

sources.title: New bus routes, stops coming to Waterfront Shopping Center

around_town.title: PennDOT Guards New Bridge During Demolition
around_town.latest_developments: PennDOT detailed the safeguards protecting the new Commercial Street bridge while crews demolish the old span during the ongoing Parkway East closure.
around_town.summary: PennDOT officials say multiple measures will keep construction work from damaging the freshly built bridge as the old structure comes down, the latest disruption from the Parkway East closure in Pittsburgh.

sources.title: How will PennDOT protect the new bridge while the old one is being demolished?

around_town.title: Veterans Breakfast Club Lands a Home
around_town.latest_developments: The Veterans Breakfast Club opened its first physical location, at the Phase Four Learning Center in Pittsburgh's Shadyside neighborhood.
around_town.summary: The nonprofit, known for hosting storytelling gatherings where veterans share their experiences, secured a permanent base after years without one.

sources.title: Veterans Breakfast Club gets first physical location in Shadyside

around_teams.title: Darnell Washington Lands Extension
around_teams.latest_developments: Steelers tight end Darnell Washington signed a four-year, $42 million extension and joined Not Just Football with Cam Heyward to talk through the deal and the season ahead.
around_teams.summary: Washington, who came to Pittsburgh as a blocking tight end with breakout upside, locked in long-term money and discussed his expanded role on the podcast hosted by teammate Cam Heyward.

sources.source: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
sources.title: Darnell Washington Joins To Talk New Extension and The Upcoming Season! | Not Just Football

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Michael Morton About E-Commerce in the Age of AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Michael Morton on how AI reshapes e-commerce, working through unfalsifiable bear cases, distribution versus referral models, grocery, and autonomous vehicles.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Exclusive: OpenAI Losses Increased Nearly 8X in 2025, With Spending Hitting $34 Billion
reading.summary: Zitron reports OpenAI's losses grew nearly eightfold in 2025 as spending reached $34 billion, arguing the company has no path to profitability.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: AI Isn’t Breaking Work. It’s Already Broken.
reading.summary: Responding to a Financial Times interview with the Work AI Institute's Rebecca Hinds and a survey of 6,000 digital workers, Newport argues AI exposes dysfunction already baked into knowledge work rather than creating it.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
